title: To A. D. - The Nightingale Has A Lyre Of Gold
content:
The nightingale has a lyre of gold,
    The lark's is a clarion-call,
    And the blackbird plays but a boxwood flute,
    But I love him best of all.

    For his song is all of the joy of life,
    And we in the mad, spring weather,
    We two have listened till he sang
    Our hearts and lips together.

    1876
meaning:
"To A. D. - The Nightingale Has A Lyre Of Gold" is a quintessential example of William Ernest Henley's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
